description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden bucket with a classic, rustic design, featuring vertical wooden staves held together by two dark metal hoops encircling its body. The bucket has two integrated wooden handles, each with a hexagonal hole for easy gripping or hanging, positioned opposite each other on the rim. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving the bucket a natural, slightly weathered appearance, while the metal bands provide structural reinforcement. The interior is open and hollow, suitable for carrying or holding liquids or small items. The bucket's construction suggests it is both functional and decorative, commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as a utility item in garages. There are no visible labels, markings, or decorative elements beyond the natural wood and metal features.
description: A rustic wooden bucket with two integrated wooden handles and dark metal reinforcement bands, featuring visible wood grain and an open interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, and both integrated wooden handles with hexagonal holes.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the thickness of the wooden rim and the side profile of the handles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side of the bucket, with both handles visible and the metal bands clearly seen.
description_view_3: Angled rear view emphasizing the bucket's depth, the curvature of the staves, and the handle structure.
